source: Limitless (Bankless) — AI supercycle; Unity as world-model builder; chips/memory/power
summary: Positron (inference chip startup) is a portfolio bet on the inference compute buildout
summary_extended: Baker holds Positron, a private inference chip startup, as a concentrated portfolio bet on the structural shift in AI compute spend from pre-training to post-training and reasoning workloads. He describes Positron as similar in thesis to Cerebras — both companies design silicon optimized specifically for inference rather than training, targeting the emerging demand as AI labs deploy models at scale. Baker's view is that inference-optimized chips represent a major growth area distinct from the GPU-dominated training market, and that purpose-built inference silicon will capture meaningful share as reasoning workloads proliferate. Because Positron is a private company with no US-listed equity, there are no publicly tradeable tickers directly associated with this specific thesis. The investment implication is primarily relevant to private market participants; no public equity plays are unambiguously implied by this specific holding.
detail: Baker holds Positron, an inference chip company similar in thesis to Cerebras, as part of his concentrated bet that inference-optimized silicon will be a major growth area as AI labs shift spend from pre-training to post-training and reasoning workloads.
confidence_evidence: Named as a portfolio holding; thesis is consistent with broader inference theme but company is early-stage/private
quote_fragment: Positron creates inference chips, very similar to Cerebras
